analyses.name: Liquidia Corporation
analyses.verdict: Top-scored setup in today's scan (94.8) -- a rare-disease pharma at a fresh 52-week high on its launch-drug ramp, with TradingView's strongest possible rating confirming.

financials.summary: Liquidia's growth is almost entirely the Yutrepia (inhaled treprostinil) launch, approved for pulmonary arterial hypertension and PH-ILD. Revenue has scaled off a near-zero base as the launch ramps, and the scan shows revenue up roughly 40x YoY off that low comparison base -- a launch-year inflection, not steady-state growth. The company had been loss-making pre-launch and is now working toward operating leverage as prescriptions build.
highlights: Revenue up ~4,150% YoY per scan data -- the Yutrepia launch ramp off a near-zero base
Forward P/E of 14.6x -- inexpensive for a commercial-stage launch story if the ramp holds
Ended a multi-year patent/regulatory fight with United Therapeutics over Tyvaso DPI, clearing the runway for Yutrepia
Building out its own commercial salesforce for the PAH/PH-ILD indications

accomplishments: Full commercial launch of Yutrepia (inhaled treprostinil) for PAH and PH-ILD
Resolved long-running patent litigation with United Therapeutics, removing a multi-year overhang
Built out a dedicated specialty-pulmonology sales organization

journey_52w.summary: LQDA spent much of the past year working through the final stages of its patent dispute with United Therapeutics while ramping the Yutrepia launch; as the legal overhang cleared and prescription data started showing durable uptake, the stock re-rated sharply, culminating in today's breakout to a fresh 52-week high on a tight 4-contraction base.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Yutrepia commercial launch ramp following FDA approval
United Therapeutics patent litigation resolved, clearing a multi-year legal overhang

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock builds a tight 4-contraction VCP and breaks to a new 52-week high on volume dry-up, RS line confirming new leadership

risks: Single-product revenue concentration -- Yutrepia is effectively the whole investment case
16.2% short interest with 6.2 days-to-cover -- volatile in both directions
Small-cap ($7.2B) biotech volatility; earnings in 36 days is a real gap-risk event
catalysts: Continued Yutrepia prescription/refill data in upcoming quarters
Potential label expansion or new indication data
Earnings print in ~36 days -- first real read on launch durability beyond the initial ramp
watch: Prescription trend data (NRx/TRx) as the best leading indicator ahead of the earnings print
Short-interest trend -- a further increase would flag skepticism building into the print
critical.valuation: 14.6x forward earnings is cheap if the launch ramp sustains, but the multiple is being applied to early-stage, still-normalizing launch economics -- treat the 'cheap' read with the usual caution around single-product launch-year comps.

analyses.name: Eli Lilly and Company
analyses.verdict: Mega-cap GLP-1 leader posting the largest EPS acceleration of any large-cap in today's scan (+170% YoY) with TradingView's unanimous STRONG BUY confirming the technical picture.

financials.summary: Lilly's results have been dominated by the continued ramp of its GLP-1 franchise (Zepbound/tirzepatide for obesity, Mounjaro for diabetes) alongside its oral GLP-1 candidate progressing through the pipeline. Revenue and EPS both accelerated sharply as manufacturing capacity caught up with historically supply-constrained demand, pushing the company to the largest market cap in the healthcare sector.
highlights: EPS growth ~170% YoY per scan data -- the standout large-cap growth number in the entire universe
Revenue growth ~55.5% YoY, driven by continued GLP-1 volume ramp
Manufacturing capacity expansions progressively easing prior supply constraints on Zepbound/Mounjaro
Forward P/E of 27.8x -- a premium multiple, but supported by the growth rate

accomplishments: Continued to scale GLP-1 manufacturing capacity to meet demand that had outstripped supply
Advanced its oral GLP-1 (orforglipron) program through late-stage development
Extended Zepbound/Mounjaro market leadership against competing GLP-1 entrants

journey_52w.summary: LLY spent the past year converting a supply-constrained demand story into deliverable revenue as manufacturing capacity came online, with the stock grinding to fresh highs as each quarter confirmed the growth was durable rather than a one-time pop; the oral GLP-1 pipeline read-throughs have added an additional layer of optionality on top of the injectable franchise.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Progressive easing of Zepbound/Mounjaro supply constraints as new manufacturing capacity came online
Continued late-stage progress on oral GLP-1 candidate orforglipron

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock confirms a new 52-week high with RS at new highs, healthcare-spending tailwind headlines (U.S. healthcare spend crossing $5.7T) reinforcing the sector story

risks: 27.8x forward P/E leaves little room for a growth disappointment
GLP-1 competitive intensity is rising (Novo Nordisk and others) -- market-share assumptions are the key swing factor
Earnings in 29 days -- pricing/access headline risk around GLP-1 reimbursement is a recurring theme for the group
catalysts: Continued Zepbound/Mounjaro volume and market-share data
Oral GLP-1 (orforglipron) regulatory and data milestones
Earnings print in ~29 days
watch: GLP-1 pricing/reimbursement policy headlines (a recurring sector-wide catalyst/risk)
Manufacturing capacity utilization commentary as a proxy for how much supply-constraint upside remains
critical.valuation: Premium multiple (27.8x forward) but backed by the largest EPS growth number of any mega-cap on this list; best treated as a core long-term holding rather than a short-term trade given the size of the position.

analyses.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
analyses.verdict: Custody-banking leader breaking out of its tightest base of the year -- cheap at 14.2x forward earnings against 41.8% EPS growth, though TradingView has no independent rating on file for this symbol.

financials.period: FY2025 (last full fiscal year, calendar Dec-end)
financials.summary: BNY's core custody, asset-servicing and markets businesses have benefited from higher rates on client cash balances plus record assets under custody/administration as markets rallied through the year; the bank has been running with improving operating leverage (fee revenue growing faster than expenses) and returning significant capital via buybacks.
highlights: EPS growth ~42% YoY per scan data -- a standout among large custody banks
Revenue growth ~13% YoY, driven by fee income and net interest income
Forward P/E of just 14.2x against that growth rate -- a below-market multiple
Continued share buybacks a meaningful EPS tailwind on top of organic growth

accomplishments: Record or near-record assets under custody/administration as markets pushed to new highs
Continued build-out of digital-assets and tokenization initiatives within custody services
Consistent capital return via dividends and buybacks

journey_52w.summary: BK has been one of the steadiest performers in the custody-bank group over the past year, grinding higher with the broader financials rally rather than any single dramatic catalyst, and is now breaking to fresh highs out of its tightest base of the last 12 months, with regional and money-center bank earnings (including BK's own) landing over the next 1-2 weeks.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Steady AUC/A growth and fee-income acceleration as markets rallied
Continued digital-assets/tokenization custody initiatives

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Breaks to new highs out of a 3-contraction VCP with RS line confirming institutional accumulation; earnings due in 8 days

risks: Earnings in 8 days -- a real binary-event gap-risk window
No independent TradingView rating available for this symbol -- no second technical opinion to cross-check
Custody-bank group (BK/NTRS/STT) is now correlated and crowded on this list
catalysts: Q2 earnings in ~8 days -- the next major catalyst
Continued AUC/A growth as a leading indicator of fee-income trajectory
Rate-path changes affecting net interest income on client cash balances
watch: Whether the RSI (currently ~60) stays above 50 on any post-earnings pullback -- a break below would undercut the uptrend thesis
The $147.29 prior-high level as the key resistance/breakout confirmation point
critical.valuation: 14.2x forward P/E against 41.8% EPS growth is a genuinely cheap multiple for the growth rate -- the best value-for-growth setup among the custody-bank trio (BK/NTRS/STT).

analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Highest-scored technology name in today's scan (94.1) -- cybersecurity demand remains the one pocket of software still working while the rest of the group lags, and TradingView confirms with a BUY.

financials.summary: Fortinet's network-security and SASE (secure access service edge) platform continued to take share as enterprises consolidate security spend onto fewer vendors; billings and subscription revenue have been the key growth drivers as the firewall-refresh cycle and SASE upsell both contributed.
highlights: Revenue growth ~20.1% YoY, EPS growth ~28.6% YoY
Forward P/E of 46.3x -- a premium multiple reflecting continued double-digit growth
SASE/secure-networking upsell into the existing firewall installed base has been a consistent growth driver

accomplishments: Continued SASE platform expansion and enterprise consolidation wins
Sustained double-digit billings growth through a period of broader software-spending caution
Maintained margin discipline while still growing revenue at a ~20% clip

journey_52w.summary: FTNT has been a relative bright spot within software over the past year as enterprise security budgets held up even as broader IT spending tightened; the stock has grinded higher on consistent execution, culminating in today's breakout to a new RS high even as most of the rest of the software group (CRM, IBM, ORCL, NOW) sits well off its highs.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Continued SASE and firewall-refresh-cycle growth even as broader software spending stayed cautious

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Confirms a VCP breakout with RS at a new high, standing out as one of the only STRONG BUY-rated names in the technology sector today

risks: 46.3x forward P/E is a premium multiple that requires continued 20%+ growth to sustain
Earnings in 23 days
Security-spend growth could decelerate if the broader enterprise IT-spending slowdown broadens
catalysts: Earnings print in ~23 days -- billings growth is the number to watch
Continued SASE attach-rate gains into the installed base
watch: Billings growth rate at the next print as the best forward indicator (more predictive than headline revenue for a subscription security vendor)
critical.valuation: Premium multiple, but justified by being one of the only technology names in today's scan combining a clean technical breakout with genuine double-digit fundamental growth.

analyses.name: Caterpillar Inc.
analyses.verdict: The sharpest quant-vs-TradingView disagreement in today's scan: a 92.1 quant score with real EPS growth (+30.2%) against an outright SELL from TradingView's independent technical read -- a WATCH, not a chase.

financials.summary: Caterpillar's construction, resource-industries and energy-and-transportation segments have benefited from continued infrastructure spending and data-center-related power-generation equipment demand, with EPS growth well ahead of revenue growth suggesting margin expansion and/or buyback-driven per-share gains on top of underlying volume growth.
highlights: EPS growth ~30.2% YoY, revenue growth ~22.2% YoY
Forward P/E of 31.1x -- a premium multiple for an industrial-cyclical name
Data-center power-generation equipment demand has been a newer growth vector alongside traditional construction/mining end-markets

accomplishments: Continued to benefit from infrastructure-spending and data-center power-generation equipment demand
Sustained double-digit revenue growth in a name historically viewed as a mature industrial cyclical
Maintained capital returns (dividends/buybacks) alongside growth investment

journey_52w.summary: CAT's past year has featured a genuine fundamental re-acceleration tied partly to data-center power-equipment demand layered on top of its traditional construction/mining cyclicality, pushing the quant score to one of the highest in the industrials group; however, the stock is currently 11.7% off its 52-week high and TradingView's technical oscillators have turned outright bearish even as the fundamental story stayed intact -- a genuine divergence between the fundamental/momentum score and the independent technical read.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: EPS and revenue both re-accelerated, aided by data-center power-generation equipment demand

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock pulls back 11.7% from its 52-week high even as the quant score stays elevated; TradingView's oscillator-based rating flips to an outright SELL (6 buy/11 sell)

risks: TradingView rates this an outright SELL (6/11/9) -- a real technical disagreement with the quant score, not a cosmetic one
31.1x forward P/E is rich if the technical picture is genuinely rolling over rather than just consolidating
Earnings in 28 days -- a binary event landing into this unresolved technical disagreement
catalysts: Earnings print in ~28 days -- the resolution point for whether the fundamental strength (EPS +30.2%) or the bearish technical read wins out
Data-center power-generation equipment order trends as an incremental growth vector
watch: Whether TradingView's technical rating improves before adding fresh capital -- this is the one name on today's list where I'd want the two signals to agree before sizing up
Price action relative to the 11.7%-off-high level -- a further breakdown would confirm the bearish technical read; a reclaim of recent highs would argue the SELL rating is stale
critical.valuation: 31.1x forward earnings for 30% EPS growth is not unreasonable on fundamentals alone, but the valuation call here depends entirely on which signal -- the quant score or TradingView's SELL -- turns out to be right; treat as unresolved.

analyses.name: Simon Property Group
analyses.verdict: Mall/retail REIT at a new high with TradingView's strongest possible rating (15/1/10) -- physical retail real estate continues to outperform amid the broader REIT rally.

financials.summary: Simon's premium mall and outlet portfolio has continued to post occupancy and releasing-spread gains as physical retail traffic stayed resilient, with funds-from-operations growth outpacing what the market typically expects from a mature mall REIT; capital recycling (selective dispositions/redevelopment) has supported the growth profile.
highlights: EPS growth ~16.4% YoY, revenue growth ~19.3% YoY -- unusually strong for a mature mall REIT
Forward P/E (FFO-multiple proxy) of 33.7x
Continued occupancy and releasing-spread strength across the premium mall/outlet portfolio

accomplishments: Sustained occupancy gains and positive releasing spreads across the mall and outlet portfolio
Continued redevelopment/mixed-use conversion of select mall properties
Maintained a strong balance sheet supporting continued capital returns

journey_52w.summary: SPG has been a standout within the REIT group over the past year as physical retail continued to prove more resilient than the e-commerce-disruption narrative implied, with occupancy and releasing spreads both trending positively; the stock has now confirmed a breakout to a fresh high alongside the broader REIT-sector rally.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Continued occupancy and releasing-spread gains across the premium mall/outlet portfolio

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Confirms a VCP breakout to a new 52-week high with RS at highs and TradingView's strongest possible technical rating

risks: 33.7x multiple is rich for a REIT even with the growth
Earnings in 35 days
Consumer discretionary spending softness would flow directly into mall traffic and releasing spreads
catalysts: Earnings print in ~35 days -- occupancy and releasing-spread trends are the key metrics
Continued redevelopment monetization
watch: Retail sales / consumer spending data as a leading indicator for occupancy trends
critical.valuation: Rich multiple for the asset class, but supported by genuinely above-average FFO growth for a mature mall REIT -- the cleanest large-cap REIT setup in today's scan.

analyses.name: Match Group, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Dating-app consolidator at a new high with the cheapest forward multiple (9.3x) of any pick on today's list against real EPS growth (+52%) -- TradingView's strongest possible rating confirms.

financials.summary: Match Group's portfolio (Tinder, Hinge, and other dating brands) has focused on monetization improvements and cost discipline rather than pure user-growth, with EPS growing far faster than revenue -- consistent with a margin-expansion and buyback-driven earnings story on top of modest top-line growth.
highlights: EPS growth ~52.0% YoY -- driven primarily by margin expansion and buybacks rather than revenue growth
Revenue growth a modest ~3.9% YoY
Forward P/E of just 9.3x -- the cheapest multiple of any pick on today's entire list

accomplishments: Continued cost-discipline and margin-expansion initiatives across the portfolio
Hinge continuing to be a relative growth bright spot within the broader portfolio
Sustained share buyback program supporting per-share earnings growth

journey_52w.summary: MTCH spent the past year working through a maturation phase in its core Tinder user base while leaning on Hinge growth and portfolio-wide cost discipline to drive earnings well ahead of revenue; the market has increasingly rewarded the margin story, with the stock confirming a breakout to a new high on this scan.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Continued margin-expansion and buyback-driven EPS growth despite modest revenue growth

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Confirms a VCP breakout to a new 52-week high with RS at highs and TradingView's unanimous STRONG BUY read

risks: Revenue growth of only ~3.9% means the EPS growth is a margin/buyback story, not a demand story -- less durable if buyback pace slows
Earnings in 29 days
Core Tinder user-base maturation remains the structural overhang on the whole portfolio
catalysts: Earnings print in ~29 days -- Hinge growth trajectory and overall paying-user trends are the key metrics
Continued buyback pace as an EPS tailwind
watch: Hinge user and revenue growth specifically, as the best-performing brand in the portfolio
Paying-user trends at Tinder as the read on whether the maturation has stabilized or is still declining
critical.valuation: 9.3x forward earnings for 52% EPS growth is the single cheapest growth multiple in today's entire pick list -- attractive if the margin story is durable, though it's worth confirming that's not solely a low-quality, buyback-driven number.

analyses.name: Sirius XM Holdings
analyses.verdict: TradingView confirms with a BUY (15/5/6) and the fundamental setup is real, but RSI 89.2 is the single most overbought reading in today's entire scan -- a WATCH for a pullback, not a fresh chase.

financials.summary: Sirius XM's satellite-radio subscription business has focused on cost discipline and subscriber-retention initiatives following the Liberty Media split-off/tracking-stock combination, with EPS growth substantially outpacing modest revenue growth -- again a margin/buyback-driven earnings story more than a demand-growth one.
highlights: EPS growth ~21.8% YoY
Revenue growth roughly flat (~1.1% YoY) -- earnings growth is being driven by margin and share-count, not subscriber growth
Forward P/E of just 9.1x -- one of the cheapest multiples in today's pick list

accomplishments: Completed the post-Liberty Media corporate restructuring, simplifying the ownership/tracking-stock structure
Continued cost-discipline initiatives to support margin expansion despite flat revenue
Sustained buyback activity supporting per-share earnings growth

journey_52w.summary: SIRI spent the past year completing its corporate restructuring away from the Liberty Media tracking-stock structure while working to stabilize a largely flat subscriber base through cost discipline; the stock has run hard into today's scan, pushing RSI to 89.2 -- the most extended reading of any name in the entire universe scanned today.

milestones.note: Completed the Liberty Media split-off / corporate restructuring

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock runs to a new 52-week high with RSI reaching 89.2 -- the most overbought reading of any ticker in today's full scan

risks: RSI 89.2 -- extreme overbought reading, high odds of a near-term pullback or consolidation
Revenue growth essentially flat -- the EPS growth is not backed by subscriber/demand growth
Earnings in 23 days landing into an already-extended stock is a poor risk/reward combination
catalysts: Earnings print in ~23 days
Subscriber trend data as the key read on whether the flat-revenue trend is stabilizing or still declining
watch: RSI normalizing toward the 60-70 range before adding fresh capital
Subscriber-count trend at the next earnings print
critical.valuation: 9.1x forward earnings is cheap on paper, but the cheapness reflects the market's skepticism about a structurally flat-to-declining subscriber base -- the current price extension (RSI 89.2) is a bigger near-term risk than the valuation is an opportunity.

analyses.name: Innovative Industrial Properties
analyses.verdict: Small-cap cannabis-focused REIT with a confirmed VCP breakout and TradingView BUY backing -- the cheapest REIT (12.9x) on today's list but with real tenant-concentration risk.

financials.summary: IIPR's sale-leaseback model with state-licensed cannabis cultivators has faced tenant-credit headwinds as several operators in the space have struggled financially, leading to flat-to-slightly-negative trailing revenue and earnings even as the stock has technically broken out on renewed sentiment around federal cannabis-policy reform prospects.
highlights: Trailing EPS roughly flat (~0.0% YoY), revenue roughly flat-to-down (~-3.8% YoY) -- tenant-credit issues have weighed on trailing numbers
Forward P/E of 12.9x -- the cheapest REIT in today's pick list
Small-cap scale ($1.8B) with a concentrated, cannabis-sector-specific tenant base

accomplishments: Continued to manage tenant defaults/re-leasing within the cannabis-cultivator portfolio
Maintained dividend payments despite sector-wide tenant-credit stress
Selectively re-let or repurposed properties from underperforming tenants

journey_52w.summary: IIPR spent much of the past year managing through tenant-credit stress across its cannabis-cultivator lease book, with trailing financials roughly flat as a result; renewed optimism around potential federal cannabis rescheduling/reform has been a periodic sentiment driver, and the stock has now confirmed a VCP breakout to a fresh high despite the still-flat trailing fundamentals.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Managed tenant-credit stress and re-leasing activity across the cannabis-cultivator portfolio

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Confirms a VCP breakout with RS at new highs despite flat trailing financials -- a sentiment/technical-led move

risks: Tenant-concentration risk in a single, federally-illegal-at-the-national-level industry is the core structural risk
Flat-to-negative trailing revenue/EPS -- the breakout is running ahead of the fundamentals
Federal cannabis-policy headlines (rescheduling, banking reform) can move this stock sharply in either direction on rumor alone
catalysts: Any federal cannabis rescheduling or banking-reform (SAFE Banking Act) progress
Tenant re-leasing/collection updates at the next earnings print
watch: Tenant-rent-collection commentary at the next print as the key fundamental health check
Federal cannabis-policy news flow given the outsized sentiment impact on this specific name
critical.valuation: 12.9x forward earnings is cheap, but appropriately so given the flat trailing fundamentals and concentrated tenant risk -- size smaller than the diversified REIT picks (SPG, PSA) given the sector-specific risk.

analyses.name: SpaceX
analyses.verdict: Recent IPO with too little trading history for the quant scan or TradingView to rate meaningfully (both show thin/weak reads on a handful of days of data) -- the investment case here is entirely the underlying business and IPO-momentum story, not the technicals.

financials.period: Pre-IPO / early public trading -- limited public financial history
financials.summary: SpaceX's business is built on two pillars: the Falcon 9/Falcon Heavy/Starship launch business (increasingly reusable, driving down cost-per-launch and capturing the large majority of global commercial and government launch volume) and Starlink, the satellite-broadband constellation that has scaled into a large, fast-growing subscription revenue base and is reportedly a significant and growing share of total company revenue and profitability. As a very recent listing, only a handful of trading days of public price history exist, so standard technical signals (VCP, RS, moving averages) are not yet meaningful.
highlights: Falcon 9 dominates global commercial/government launch cadence with industry-leading reusability economics
Starlink has scaled to a large, fast-growing satellite-broadband subscriber base and is a major, growing revenue contributor
Starship development continues toward full reusability and higher payload capacity, the long-term platform for Mars/deep-space ambitions
As a new listing (is_new_listing flag), 52-week/VCP/RS breakout signals are not yet resolvable -- expect several months before the standard technical toolkit becomes meaningful

accomplishments: Continued high-cadence Falcon 9 launches with reused boosters, the core cost advantage over competitors
Starlink subscriber and revenue scale continuing to grow rapidly, increasingly diversifying the business beyond pure launch services
Advanced Starship toward higher flight cadence and reusability milestones
Completed its public listing, providing a new liquid vehicle for investors to access the business

journey_52w.summary: As a brand-new listing, SPCX does not have a meaningful 52-week public trading history yet -- the relevant 'journey' is pre-IPO: continued Falcon 9 launch-cadence dominance, Starlink's scale-up into a major standalone revenue driver, and Starship's development progress, culminating in the recent public listing that this scan is now tracking from day one.

milestones.label: Pre-IPO
milestones.note: Falcon 9 launch cadence and reusability continued to set the industry cost benchmark

milestones.label: Pre-IPO
milestones.note: Starlink scaled into a major, fast-growing revenue and subscriber base

milestones.label: Recent
milestones.note: Public listing completed; stock now trades with only a handful of days of price history, hence the 'NEW' flag and unresolved technical signals

risks: Extremely limited public trading history -- normal technical risk-management tools (stops based on moving averages, VCP-based entries) are not yet reliable
IPO-era volatility is typically elevated in both directions as price discovery occurs
Government/regulatory dependence (NASA, DoD launch contracts; FCC/spectrum policy for Starlink) is a structural risk for both business lines
catalysts: First few quarters of public financial disclosure, which will give the market real comparables for the first time
Continued Starship flight-test milestones
Starlink subscriber-growth and revenue-mix disclosures as the market gets its first real look at that segment's standalone economics
watch: First earnings report as a public company -- the first real fundamental data point
How the stock behaves as the initial IPO lock-up and early-trading volatility settles
Starship flight-test cadence and outcomes given how much of the long-term thesis rests on it
critical.valuation: Not meaningfully assessable yet on standard public-market metrics given the trading history -- treat this as a thesis-driven, business-quality holding for now rather than a technically-timed one; avoid sizing based on the (currently unreliable) quant score or TV's thin-data SELL read.
